comment: Synthetic primary-refractory DLBCL post 1L R-CHOP (PD at 4 mo, treatment stopped) → 2L Pola-R-CHP (transient PR then PD at 3 mo). Age 71, ECOG 2, frail, multiple grade-2 comorbidities; not transplant-eligible AND not CAR-T-eligible (no UA international referral pathway). Entering 3L — textbook indication is loncastuximab tesirine (LOTIS-2 Caimi 2021 Lancet Oncol; ORR 48%, CR 24%, mPFS 4.9 mo, mOS 9.5 mo) — off-the-shelf outpatient CD19-targeted ADC ideally suited for non-CAR-T-eligible frail patient. Engine routing (2026-05-10 fix): line_of_therapy=3 → ALGO-DLBCL-3L step 1 → RF-DLBCL-CART-INELIGIBLE-POST-2L fires (cart_ineligibility_reason set + prior_lines_count=2 ≥2) → IND-DLBCL-3L-LONCASTUXIMAB (standard track, default). Alternative aggressive track = IND-DLBCL-3L-AXICEL-CART (CAR-T, not accessible for this patient). Note: previously line_of_therapy was set to 2 as a workaround pending ALGO-DLBCL-3L authoring; that caused IND-DLBCL-3L-LONCASTUXIMAB to be filtered by the line-mismatch guard and the engine fell back to IND-DLBCL-2L-POLA-R-BENDAMUSTINE — clinically inappropriate (patient already failed Pola-based 2L Pola-R-CHP; re-treating same MMAE class is futile + grade-1 neuropathy present).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, NOS — primary-refractory after Pola-R-CHP
primary_diagnosis.who_classification_5th_ed: Aggressive B-cell lymphomas — Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, NOS

transplant_assessment.rationale: Age 71, ECOG 2, mild renal impairment (CrCl 58) and grade 1 neuropathy → autoSCT not advised

cart_assessment.rationale: Frailty (age 71, ECOG 2, comorbidity grade-2 count 3); no funded UA international CAR-T referral pathway secured; manufacturing window risk high given primary-refractory disease
